title: Will Trent shares rebound after Q1 update triggers 13% crash? Here's what technical charts indicate
summary: After the 13% crash, Trent shares have technically slipped below their 20-day EMA, while the RSI has witnessed a sharp downtick, signalling a shift in momentum from bullish to bearish, said Sudeep Shah, Head of Technical and Derivatives Research at SBI Securities

title: Titan dividend alert! Last day to buy shares for Rs 15 dividend payout. Do you own?
summary: Only those shareholders who own Titan shares in their demat accounts as of Thursday will be eligible to receive the bonus shares. Under the market regulator SEBI's T+1 settlement cycle, investors must buy shares of a company at least one trading day before the record date so that they are credited to their demat accounts by that day, making them eligible for the dividend.

title: Negative Breakout: These 11 stocks cross below their 200 DMAs
summary: The 200 DMA is used as a key indicator by traders for determining the overall trend in a particular stock.

title: Knack Packaging IPO shares to debut today. Here's what GMP suggests ahead of listing
summary: Ahead of the listing, the grey market is indicating a positive debut. The IPO is currently commanding a Grey Market Premium (GMP) of around Rs 16 per share, suggesting an estimated listing price of Rs 186, about 10% higher than the upper end of the IPO price band of Rs 170.

title: D-Street set for a negative opening as GIFT Nifty trades lower
summary: Domestic markets ended lower after a four-session winning streak on Tuesday. Profit booking and weakness in Realty and Metals stocks impacted market sentiment. Foreign portfolio investors bought shares, while domestic institutional investors sold them. The Indian rupee saw its biggest single-session gain in three weeks. Investor focus remains on business updates and trade agreement progress.

title: General insurers clock 16.7% premium growth
summary: The general insurance sector saw a 16.7% premium income rise in June.  Standalone health insurers led this growth with a 31% increase.  First quarter collections for FY27 also climbed by 11.1%.  Niva Bupa Health Insurance showed strong premium growth of 34% in June.  ICICI Lombard General Insurance reported a 13.7% premium increase for the month.

title: Depositories get to spend 5% of IPF interest income
summary: Sebi has revised rules for depositories' Investor Protection Fund income.  Depositories can now use five percent of annual income for expenses.  The remaining ninety-five percent must be reinvested back into the fund.  This change allows for administrative costs like salaries and audit fees.  Any unutilized funds from the five percent must be returned to the fund.

title: Malabar Group companies' merger gets CCI nod
summary: The Competition Commission of India approved the merger of fifty-one Malabar Group companies. These companies are primarily engaged in the jewellery business sector. Separately, the regulator dismissed a case against Nissan Motor India. The complainant had accused Nissan of anti-competitive dealership agreement termination practices. The CCI found no violation of competition rules in the Nissan case.

title: Stocks in news: Adani Enterprises, Cochin Shipyard, IDFC First Bank, Premier Energies, Glenmark Pharma
summary: Markets traded subdued on the weekly expiry day, with indices ending marginally lower. Cochin Shipyard's offer for sale saw strong investor demand on its first day. Adani Enterprises' US legal case faces a likely dismissal by federal prosecutors. IDFC First Bank launched provident fund payment services through its EPFO integration. Premier Energies secured significant orders, and Glenmark Pharma launched a generic drug in the US.

title: Trent slides 12% as Q1 growth fails to impress
summary: Trent shares experienced a significant single-day decline on Tuesday.  This drop followed first-quarter financial results that did not meet market expectations.  Revenue growth was largely driven by recently opened stores in smaller towns.  These new locations show lower revenue per square foot compared to metros.  Analysts remain optimistic about Trent's long-term growth prospects.

title: Cochin Shipyard OFS gets 3.5x bids; govt exercises greenshoe
summary: Cochin Shipyard's Offer for Sale saw strong demand from non-retail investors.  The issue was subscribed 3.52 times on the first day of bidding.  This robust interest prompted the government to utilize the entire greenshoe option.  The Offer for Sale will now open for retail investors and employees.  The floor price for this share offering was set at ₹1,400.

title: Cost of free alerts: RBI’s SMS order may dent banks’ income by Rs 300 crore
summary: Banks have also been given the flexibility of dropping mandatory alerts for lowvalue transactions. However, top-draw private banks, such as HDFC Bank or ICICI Bank, are unlikely to reduce SMS alerts for such transactions to ensure their reputation for technology adoption and customer experience isn’t compromised.

title: Irdai asks state-run insurers to account for wage costs annually
summary: Irdai has implemented a new requirement for state insurers to create annual provisions for wage increases, intending to soften the financial repercussions of periodic wage negotiations. Insurers are now obliged to evaluate the sufficiency of these provisions quarterly after obligations become clear. The government has ruled out options for additional capital, instead banking on solvency improvements from IFRS 17 and stakes in the NSE.

title: ARC consolidation: Govt weighs ASREC merger with NARCL
summary: The government is considering a merger of ASREC Asset Reconstruction Company with NARCL to enhance the functionality of state-sponsored bad-loan resolution efforts. This proposal, still in preliminary discussions, underscores a strategic effort for consolidation rather than growth. ASREC ARC is majorly held by public sector lenders and LIC, aiming for better management and scrutiny of bad loans.

title: RBI approves re-appointment of NS Vishwanathan as Axis Bank's non-executive chairman
summary: The Reserve Bank of India has approved NS Vishwanathan's reappointment as Axis Bank's non-executive chairman. His new three-year term will commence from October 27, 2026, and conclude on October 26, 2029. This extension is contingent upon his reappointment as an independent director for the bank. Vishwanathan, a former deputy governor, has been serving as part-time chairman since October 2023.

title: NBFC credit grows 14% in May, led by retail and farm loans
summary: Loans against gold jewellery remained the fastest-growing segment, surging 70% year-on-year to ₹3.29 lakh crore in May, compared with 39% growth a year earlier.

title: Promoter caution makes investment banks work longer and harder for IPO wins
summary: Winning an IPO mandate is becoming a longer process for investment banks. Cautious promoters are extending IPO preparations, doing deeper due diligence and taking more time to choose advisers, reflecting a shift in how companies approach public market listings.

title: Google will set up more AI data centres in India as demand grows, says cloud business CEO Thomas Kurian
summary: A multi-year capital expansion plan will follow the $15-billion investment in Andhra Pradesh announced last October, Thomas Kurian, chief executive of Google Cloud, told Mint.

title: Rapido has more users than Uber and Ola. And Swiggy, Zomato, Zepto, Blinkit
summary: Rapido averaged 82 million monthly active users between March and May, up 67% year-on-year. That put it ahead of Uber (39 million), Ola (27 million), Blinkit (79 million), Swiggy (67 million) and Zomato (63 million).

title: New GIC Re chief banks on AI, resilience for next growth phase
summary: The state-owned reinsurer is building 'Ragini', a private AI assistant, to capture underwriting expertise before it walks out the door. New chairman and managing director Hitesh Joshi talks to Mint about AI, portfolio rebalancing and the push for an 'A' rating.

title: 8th Pay Commission: Can salaries rise by over 60%? Here's how HRA, TA & DA proposals may boost pay for level 1 payments
summary: The 8th Pay Commission could significantly increase central government salaries if proposals on fitment factor, HRA, TA and DA merger are accepted. While estimates suggest a 60% rise in some cases, the government is yet to take a final decision.
